name: User Journey Mapping
description: Map the complete user journey step by step
when_to_use: Understand the current experience before any modification

name: Competitive UI Audit
description: Analyze the UI of 3-5 direct competitors to identify standards
when_to_use: Position yourself and discover domain conventions

name: Heuristic Evaluation
description: Evaluate the interface against Nielsen's 10 heuristics
when_to_use: Detect usability issues without user testing

name: Accessibility Audit WCAG
description: Verify compliance with WCAG 2.1 criteria (A, AA, AAA)
when_to_use: Before delivery or when accessibility is required

name: Atomic Design
description: Organize components into atoms, molecules, organisms, templates, pages
when_to_use: Design a modular and reusable design system

name: Design Tokens
description: Define the fundamental variables: colors, typography, spacing, shadows
when_to_use: Centralize design values for consistency and maintainability

name: Mood Board
description: Gather visual references to define the ambiance before coding
when_to_use: Align the team on the visual direction without over-specifying

name: Progressive Disclosure
description: Reveal information progressively according to the user's need
when_to_use: Simplify complex interfaces without removing options

name: Card Sorting
description: Have users sort content to validate the information architecture
when_to_use: Define or validate navigation and categorization

name: Wireframe Low-Fidelity
description: Sketch page structures without visual detail
when_to_use: Quickly validate the layout before investing in design
